percentage-calculator.title: Percentage Calculator
percentage-calculator.description: Calculate percentages, percentage increase, decrease, and find what percent one number is of another.

content.introduction: Find a percentage of a number, determine what percent one value is of another, or calculate percentage increase and decrease.

howToSteps.name: Choose a mode
howToSteps.text: Select X% of Y, X is what % of Y, or percent change between two values.

howToSteps.name: Enter values
howToSteps.text: Type the two numbers required for your selected mode.

howToSteps.name: Read the result
howToSteps.text: The calculated percentage and a plain-language label appear below.

examples.title: 20% of 150
examples.description: Mode: X% of Y. Enter 20 and 150 to get 30.

examples.title: Percent change
examples.description: From 80 to 100 is a 25% increase.

faqs.question: Can I calculate percent increase and decrease?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use the percent change tab and enter the original and new values.

faqs.question: What if the second number is zero?
faqs.answer: Division by zero is undefined. The calculator returns no result when the denominator is zero.

faqs.question: Is this tool free?
faqs.answer: Yes. No account or payment is required.

simple-interest-calculator.title: Simple Interest Calculator
simple-interest-calculator.description: Calculate simple interest and total amount from principal, rate, and time.

content.introduction: Calculate simple interest and total amount from principal, annual rate, and time in years.

howToSteps.name: Enter principal
howToSteps.text: Type the initial loan or investment amount.

howToSteps.name: Set rate and time
howToSteps.text: Enter annual interest rate (%) and duration in years.

howToSteps.name: View results
howToSteps.text: See interest earned and total amount.

examples.title: $10,000 at 5% for 3 years
examples.description: Principal 10000, rate 5, time 3 yields $1,500 interest and $11,500 total.

faqs.question: What is simple interest?
faqs.answer: Simple interest is calculated only on the original principal, not on accumulated interest.

faqs.question: When should I use compound interest instead?
faqs.answer: Use compound interest when earnings are reinvested and interest accrues on prior interest.

faqs.question: Is this tool free?
faqs.answer: Yes. No account is required.

discount-calculator.title: Discount Calculator
discount-calculator.description: Calculate final price and savings from original price and discount percentage.

content.introduction: Find the final sale price and total savings from an original price and discount percentage.

howToSteps.name: Enter original price
howToSteps.text: Type the full price before discount.

howToSteps.name: Enter discount percent
howToSteps.text: Type the discount as a percentage (e.g. 20 for 20% off).

howToSteps.name: See savings
howToSteps.text: View final price and amount saved.

examples.title: 20% off $100
examples.description: Original price 100 with 20% discount gives a final price of $80 and $20 saved.

faqs.question: Can I enter discounts over 100%?
faqs.answer: No. Discounts are limited to 0–100% for valid results.

faqs.question: Does this include tax?
faqs.answer: No. Tax is calculated on the final price separately depending on your region.

faqs.question: Is this tool free?
faqs.answer: Yes.

examples.title: Test scores
examples.description: Enter 85, 90, 78, 92, 88 to get mean 86.6 and median 88.

faqs.question: How is the mode calculated?
faqs.answer: The mode is the most frequently occurring value. Multiple modes may be listed.

faqs.question: What number formats are accepted?
faqs.answer: Integers and decimals separated by commas, spaces, or line breaks.

faqs.question: Is this tool free?
faqs.answer: Yes.

emi-calculator.description: Calculate your monthly EMI for home loans, car loans, and personal loans.

howToSteps.name: Enter loan amount
howToSteps.text: Type the principal loan amount.

howToSteps.text: Enter annual interest rate and loan tenure in years or months.

howToSteps.name: View EMI

examples.title: Home loan example
examples.description: ₹50,00,000 at 8.5% for 20 years gives a monthly EMI you can compare across lenders.

faqs.question: What is EMI?
faqs.answer: EMI (Equated Monthly Installment) is a fixed payment toward principal and interest each month.

faqs.question: Does this include fees or insurance?
faqs.answer: No. This calculator covers principal and interest only. Add processing fees separately.

faqs.question: Is this tool free?
faqs.answer: Yes. No account or payment is required.

faqs.answer: Annually, semi-annually, quarterly, monthly, and daily.

faqs.question: Are taxes or fees included?
faqs.answer: No. Results show gross growth before taxes, fees, or withdrawals.

faqs.question: Is this tool free?
faqs.answer: Yes. No signup is required.

faqs.question: What hours per week are assumed?
faqs.answer: Hourly rate assumes a 40-hour work week and 52 weeks per year.

faqs.question: Is tax deducted?
faqs.answer: No. Figures are gross (pre-tax) salary conversions.

faqs.question: Is this tool free?
faqs.answer: Yes.

faqs.question: Is tip calculated before or after tax?
faqs.answer: Tip is calculated on the bill amount you enter (typically pre-tax).

bmi-calculator.title: BMI Calculator
bmi-calculator.description: Calculate your Body Mass Index and see your weight category based on height and weight.

content.introduction: Calculate your Body Mass Index (BMI) instantly from height and weight. Supports metric and imperial units with a visual category indicator.

howToSteps.name: Enter weight
howToSteps.text: Type your weight and choose kilograms or pounds.

howToSteps.name: Enter height
howToSteps.text: Type your height and choose centimeters or feet.

howToSteps.name: Read your BMI
howToSteps.text: Your BMI value and weight category appear in the results section.

examples.title: Adult metric example
examples.description: Weight 70 kg and height 175 cm gives a BMI of about 22.9 (normal weight).

examples.title: Imperial example
examples.description: Weight 154 lbs and height 5 ft 9 in converts to metric internally before calculating BMI.

faqs.question: What is a healthy BMI range?
faqs.answer: For most adults, a BMI between 18.5 and 24.9 is considered normal weight. Below 18.5 is underweight; 25–29.9 is overweight; 30 and above is obese.

faqs.question: Is BMI accurate for athletes?
faqs.answer: BMI does not distinguish muscle from fat. Athletes with high muscle mass may have a high BMI despite low body fat.

faqs.question: Is my health data sent to a server?
faqs.answer: No. BMI calculation can run in your browser. The public API is available for agents and validates the same formula server-side.

content.article: Body Mass Index (BMI) is a widely used screening measure for weight categories. It is calculated as weight in kilograms divided by height in meters squared. While useful for population-level trends, BMI should be interpreted alongside other health factors.

calorie-calculator.title: Calorie Calculator
calorie-calculator.description: Estimate daily calorie needs based on age, weight, height, and activity level.

content.introduction: Estimate daily calorie needs (BMR and TDEE) using the Mifflin-St Jeor equation and activity level.

howToSteps.name: Enter body stats
howToSteps.text: Provide age, gender, weight (kg), and height (cm).

howToSteps.name: Select activity level
howToSteps.text: Choose how active you are day to day.

howToSteps.name: View calorie targets
howToSteps.text: See maintenance, weight loss, and weight gain targets.

examples.title: Moderately active adult
examples.description: A 30-year-old at 70 kg and 175 cm with moderate activity gets a TDEE estimate for meal planning.

faqs.question: What formula is used?
faqs.answer: The Mifflin-St Jeor equation for BMR, multiplied by an activity factor for TDEE.

faqs.question: Are these medical recommendations?
faqs.answer: No. Results are estimates. Consult a healthcare provider for personalized nutrition advice.

faqs.question: Is this tool free?
faqs.answer: Yes.

ideal-weight-calculator.title: Ideal Weight Calculator
ideal-weight-calculator.description: Find your ideal body weight range based on height using multiple formulas.

content.introduction: Estimate ideal body weight using Devine, Robinson, Miller, and Hamwi formulas based on height and gender.

howToSteps.name: Enter height
howToSteps.text: Type your height in centimeters.

howToSteps.name: Select gender
howToSteps.text: Choose male or female for formula selection.

howToSteps.name: Compare formulas
howToSteps.text: Review each formula result and the suggested weight range.

examples.title: 175 cm male
examples.description: Compare four clinical formulas to see a recommended weight range in kg and lbs.

faqs.question: Which formula is most accurate?
faqs.answer: Each formula was developed for different populations. The range across all four is often more useful than any single result.

faqs.question: Does this account for muscle mass?
faqs.answer: No. Athletes and muscular individuals may weigh more than these estimates suggest.

faqs.question: Is this tool free?
faqs.answer: Yes.

faqs.question: Can I calculate age as of a past date?
faqs.answer: Yes. Change the as-of date to compute age on any historical date.

faqs.question: Why does my age show months and days?
faqs.answer: Partial years are expressed as remaining months and days for precision beyond whole years.

faqs.question: Is this tool free?
faqs.answer: Yes. No signup is required.

faqs.question: How is reading time calculated?
faqs.answer: Reading time assumes roughly 200 words per minute, a common editorial estimate.

faqs.question: Is my text stored?
faqs.answer: Text is sent to the Toolsify API for analysis and is not stored.

faqs.answer: Capitalizes the first letter of each major word, common for headings.

faqs.question: Does camelCase handle spaces?
faqs.answer: Yes. Words are split on spaces and punctuation before casing rules are applied.

howToSteps.name: Review matches
howToSteps.text: See highlighted matches and capture group details below.

examples.title: Validate an email pattern
examples.description: Use the Email preset pattern and paste sample addresses to see which match.

faqs.question: What regex flavor does this use?
faqs.answer: This tool uses JavaScript regular expressions, the same flavor supported by modern browsers and Node.js.

faqs.question: Does my data leave my browser?
faqs.answer: Pattern matching is processed through the Toolsify API. Avoid pasting confidential text if that is a concern.

faqs.question: What do the regex flags mean?
faqs.answer: g = global, i = case-insensitive, m = multiline, s = dotAll, u = unicode.

json-formatter.title: JSON Formatter & Validator
json-formatter.description: Format, minify, validate JSON data with a collapsible tree view and syntax highlighting.

content.introduction: Format, minify, and validate JSON with a collapsible tree view and syntax-aware error messages.

howToSteps.name: Paste JSON
howToSteps.text: Paste raw JSON into the editor.

howToSteps.name: Format or minify
howToSteps.text: Click Format for readable output or Minify for a single line.

howToSteps.name: Inspect the tree
howToSteps.text: Expand nodes in the tree view to explore nested structures.

examples.title: Pretty-print an API response
examples.description: Paste a compact API JSON payload and click Format to make it readable.

faqs.question: Is this safe for sensitive JSON data?
faqs.answer: JSON is processed through the Toolsify API to format and validate. Do not paste secrets you cannot send to a server.

faqs.question: Can I minify JSON here too?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use the Minify button to remove whitespace and produce a compact single-line JSON string.

faqs.question: Will invalid JSON show where the error is?
faqs.answer: When possible, the validator reports the parse error message along with an approximate line and column.

jwt-decoder.title: JWT Decoder & Inspector
jwt-decoder.description: Decode JWT header and payload, check expiry, and inspect claims instantly.

content.introduction: Decode JSON Web Token headers and payloads, inspect claims, and check expiry — entirely in your browser.

howToSteps.name: Paste a JWT
howToSteps.text: Paste a three-part JWT string into the input field.

howToSteps.name: Review decoded parts
howToSteps.text: Inspect the header, payload, and signature sections.

howToSteps.name: Check time claims
howToSteps.text: Look at exp, iat, and nbf for issuance and expiry timing.

examples.title: Debug a session token
examples.description: Paste an access token to confirm audience, issuer, and expiration before troubleshooting auth.

faqs.question: Is my token uploaded anywhere?
faqs.answer: Inputs are sent to the Toolsify API for decoding and are not stored. For maximum privacy, use tokens you are comfortable processing through an online service.

faqs.question: Does this verify my JWT signature?
faqs.answer: No. Signature verification requires the issuer's secret or public key. This tool only decodes the header and payload.

faqs.question: Why should I be careful pasting JWTs online?
faqs.answer: JWTs can contain sensitive session data. Only decode tokens you are comfortable exposing.

cron-expression-generator.title: Cron Expression Generator
cron-expression-generator.description: Build cron expressions visually with human-readable schedule translation.

content.introduction: Build cron expressions visually with human-readable schedule descriptions and upcoming run times.

howToSteps.name: Use the builder
howToSteps.text: Set minute, hour, day, month, and weekday fields visually.

howToSteps.name: Or edit raw
howToSteps.text: Switch to Raw Input to type a five-field cron string directly.

howToSteps.name: Copy the expression
howToSteps.text: Copy the generated cron string for your scheduler or CI config.

examples.title: Every weekday at 9 AM
examples.description: Minute 0, hour 9, day-of-month *, month *, weekday 1–5 → 0 9 * * 1-5.

faqs.question: What does * mean in cron?
faqs.answer: An asterisk means every value for that field. For example, * in the minute field means every minute.

faqs.question: How do I run a job every 15 minutes?
faqs.answer: Use */15 in the minute field with the other fields set to * (for example: */15 * * * *).

faqs.question: Can I type a raw cron expression directly?
faqs.answer: Yes. Switch to Raw Input mode to edit the expression manually while keeping the visual builder in sync when possible.

gradient-generator.title: CSS Gradient Generator
gradient-generator.description: Create CSS gradients with live preview and copy-ready CSS code.

content.introduction: Create CSS linear gradients with live preview, draggable color stops, and copy-ready code.

howToSteps.name: Add color stops
howToSteps.text: Set two or more colors and their positions on the gradient.

howToSteps.name: Adjust angle
howToSteps.text: Change gradient direction in degrees.

howToSteps.name: Copy CSS
howToSteps.text: Copy the generated linear-gradient() rule for your stylesheet.

examples.title: Sunset hero background
examples.description: Blend #FF6B35 to #0058BE at 135deg for a vibrant header gradient.

faqs.question: What CSS syntax is generated?
faqs.answer: Standard linear-gradient() with degree angle and color-stop percentages.

faqs.question: Can I use more than two colors?
faqs.answer: Yes. Add multiple stops for richer multi-color gradients.

faqs.question: Is this tool free?
faqs.answer: Yes.

color-palette-generator.title: Color Palette Generator
color-palette-generator.description: Generate complementary, triadic, and analogous color palettes for design projects.

content.introduction: Generate complementary, analogous, triadic, and monochromatic palettes from a base color.

howToSteps.name: Choose a base color
howToSteps.text: Pick or type a hex color as your starting point.

howToSteps.name: Browse harmonies
howToSteps.text: Review complementary, analogous, triadic, and monochromatic sets.

howToSteps.name: Copy swatches
howToSteps.text: Click any swatch to copy its hex code.

examples.title: Design system seed color
examples.description: Start from #FF6B35 to build a cohesive palette for a landing page.

faqs.question: What is a complementary palette?
faqs.answer: Colors opposite on the color wheel — high contrast for accents and CTAs.

faqs.question: Can I randomize the base color?
faqs.answer: Yes. Use the random color control to explore new combinations.

faqs.question: Is this tool free?
faqs.answer: Yes.

hash-generator.title: Hash Generator
hash-generator.description: Generate MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, and SHA-512 hashes from any text input.

ui.filePrompt: Click or drag a file to hash
ui.fileError: Failed to read or hash the selected file.
ui.filePromptAlt: Drop or select a file to hash

content.introduction: Generate MD5, SHA-1, SHA-256, and SHA-512 hashes from text or files instantly in your browser.

howToSteps.name: Choose input mode
howToSteps.text: Select Text or File depending on what you want to hash.

howToSteps.name: Provide input
howToSteps.text: Paste text or select a file to hash.

howToSteps.name: Copy a digest
howToSteps.text: Copy any generated hash with one click.

examples.title: Checksum a string
examples.description: Paste "hello" to compare SHA-256 output against known test vectors.

faqs.question: Is MD5 secure?
faqs.answer: MD5 is no longer considered secure for passwords or digital signatures. It is still useful for quick checksums and legacy compatibility.

faqs.question: Can I hash a file instead of text?
faqs.answer: Yes. Switch to File mode and select any file. File hashing runs locally in your browser.

faqs.question: Does my data leave my browser?
faqs.answer: Text mode uses the Toolsify API. File mode hashes locally with the Web Crypto API and an in-browser MD5 implementation.

markdown-editor.title: Markdown Editor & Preview
markdown-editor.description: Write markdown in a split-pane editor with live HTML preview.

content.introduction: Write Markdown in a split-pane editor with live HTML preview powered by a secure sanitizer.

howToSteps.name: Write Markdown
howToSteps.text: Type headings, lists, links, and code blocks in the editor pane.

howToSteps.name: Preview live
howToSteps.text: See rendered HTML update as you type.

howToSteps.name: Copy output
howToSteps.text: Copy Markdown source or use the preview for documentation drafts.

examples.title: README draft
examples.description: Write a project README with headings and bullet lists, then preview before publishing.

faqs.question: Is GitHub Flavored Markdown supported?
faqs.answer: Common Markdown syntax is supported. Some GFM extensions may vary.

faqs.question: Is the preview sanitized?
faqs.answer: Yes. HTML output is sanitized before rendering to reduce XSS risk.

faqs.question: Is my content stored?
faqs.answer: No. Editing and preview happen in your browser; content is not saved on our servers.
